mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 6.0% indicates a tightening labor market that may lead to wage pressures and subsequent Fed tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a UNRATE crossing below 6.0% the S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with a low federal funds rate signal a strong housing recovery supported by accommodative policy, boosting economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the combined signal from rising HOUST and low FEDFUNDS yields negative portfolio return over 60 days in more than 40% of rolling windows, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the industrial recovery phase, an increase in industrial production indicates strengthening supply and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and stock prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods when INDPRO is above 100 and increasing, the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than half the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When retail and food services sales exceed 450,000 million dollars, it signals robust consumer demand that boosts corporate profits and equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after RSAFS exceeds 450,000 the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in over 50% of cases within a 1-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment and industrial production suggests broad-based economic strength, which drives corporate earnings higher and leads to positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return following months with both PAYEMS and INDPRO exceeding their 6-month moving averages is negative, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals expansion in manufacturing, which is positively correlated with corporate earnings and stock prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 3-year rank correlation between INDPRO momentum and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is less than 0.05, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls increase by more than 200,000 in a month, it signals a strong labor market that boosts sentiment and consumer spending, positively impacting equities over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day return after PAYEMS increases >200k is negative over any 1-year period, reject.
